summary:
The visual cortex primarily exists in the occipital lobe where it processes incoming visual information first before sending data elsewhere for further analysis. This region plays a crucial role in initiating our perception of vision. Sentence count: 2

Summary: The visual cortex mainly located in the occipital lobe handles initial visual input which then gets processed by other areas of the brain. It initiates our ability to perceive images visually. Sentence count: 2
